channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Good Weekend Quiz: Challenge Your General Knowledge
item.description: The Good Weekend magazine has released its latest general knowledge quiz, inviting readers to test their trivia skills. The quiz offers a variety of questions covering different topics, designed to ch...

item.title: Association Between Proximity to Nuclear Power Plants and Cancer Mortality in the U.S., 2000–2020
item.description: A recent national study analyzed the relationship between living near nuclear power plants and mortality from lung, breast, and colon cancers in the United States over a twenty-year period. Researcher...

item.title: Adolf Galland’s Unconventional Lobster Delivery Flight During WWII
item.description: In April 1941, German Luftwaffe ace Adolf Galland, then a Lieutenant Colonel commanding the JG 26 fighter wing, orchestrated an unusual mission: personally delivering a gourmet seafood basket to celeb...

item.title: Kenya's proposed excise duty may hurt Tanzania’s glass exports and regional trade
item.description: Tanzania’s leading glass company, KIOO Limited, don express serious concern over Kenya’s plan to impose a 35 percent excise duty on glass bottles imported from East African Community (EAC) countries. ...

item.title: Mudavadi Urges Kenyans to Prepare for Economic and Social Challenges
item.description: Prime Cabinet Secretary Musalia Mudavadi has called on Kenyans to mentally and practically prepare for upcoming economic and social challenges. Speaking in Nairobi, Mudavadi noted that the government ...

item.title: Filipino Bishops Lead National Prayer Amid Political Tensions
item.description: The Catholic Bishops' Conference of the Philippines (CBCP) has called on all parishes nationwide to recite a special prayer titled “A Nation’s Prayer for Enlightenment, Conversion and Renewal” in resp...

item.title: US Publishes Second Set of Declassified UFO Reports
item.description: The United States Defence Department has unveiled a second batch of previously classified documents relating to UFO sightings, known officially as 'unidentified anomalous phenomena'. The disclosure in...

item.title: Saudi Arabia Introduces Smart Watches to Track Heart Health of Pilgrims During Hajj
item.description: During the 2026 Hajj, Saudi Arabia don start to use smart watch technology to dey monitor pilgrims wey get heart conditions. The Madinah Health Cluster join forces with Seha Virtual Hospital put this ...

item.title: WFP PH highlights challenges to food security due to war and El Niño
item.description: The Philippines continues to face persistent threats to its food security, a situation aggravated by global crises such as the Middle East conflict. As a net food importer, the country is vulnerable t...

item.title: Brumbies secure Super Rugby finals berth with narrow win over Waratahs
item.description: The ACT Brumbies have officially secured a spot in the Super Rugby finals after a tense encounter with the NSW Waratahs in Sydney. The match saw the Brumbies fend off a late surge from the Waratahs, l...

item.title: Education Stakeholders Call for Law Update to Match New Policy Changes
item.description: Dodoma – The Ministry of Education, Science and Technology met with various education stakeholders for a two-day session to review the Education Act, Chapter 353. The review highlighted that the curre...

item.title: Understanding Motor Neurone Disease: Diagnosis Challenges and Current Treatments
item.description: Motor neurone disease (MND) is a rare but devastating neurological condition affecting fewer than 1,000 Australians each year. The disease targets motor neurones, which are nerve cells responsible for...

item.title: Lawrence Opikokew receives 13-year sentence for 2025 Saskatoon motel manslaughter
item.description: In an emotional courtroom in Saskatoon, the family of 20-year-old Matthew Brabant witnessed the sentencing of Lawrence Opikokew, who pleaded guilty to manslaughter in connection with Brabant's death d...

item.title: South Africa Sees Over 57,000 Deportations Amid Immigration Crackdown
item.description: In the 2025/26 financial year, South Africa deported 57,784 people, a significant rise from the 14,589 deported in 2020/21. Home Affairs Minister Leon Schreiber emphasised that managing undocumented i...

item.title: MMDA to Enforce Penalties on Littering Using NCAP Cameras
item.description: The Metropolitan Manila Development Authority (MMDA) is set to strictly implement penalties on individuals who litter in public spaces, using the No Contact Apprehension Policy (NCAP) cameras as part ...

item.title: Canadians deported from Gaza flotilla in Israel now in Turkey amid reports of mistreatment
item.description: Twelve Canadians who participated in the Global Sumud Flotilla attempting to deliver humanitarian aid to Gaza were detained by Israeli authorities and subsequently deported to Turkey. Foreign Affairs ...

item.title: US Bars Tanzanian Police Commander Following Alleged Human Rights Violations
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The United States has officially barred Faustine Jackson Mafwele, Senior Assistant Commissioner of the Tanzania Police Force, from entering the US. This action follows credible reports...

item.title: Agusan del Sur Prepares Free Transport, Tours, and Cultural Village for Palarong Pambansa 2026
item.description: The 66th Palarong Pambansa, scheduled from May 24 to May 31, 2026, will be hosted by Agusan del Sur, welcoming thousands of student-athletes, delegates, and spectators. In a bid to enhance the experie...

item.title: Invasive Australian Red Claw Crayfish Threaten Southern African Rivers and Fisheries
item.description: Highly invasive Australian red claw crayfish (Cherax quadricarinatus) are spreading rapidly across southern Africa, threatening local fish populations, aquatic biodiversity, and the livelihoods of sma...

item.title: Sindh Government Declares Four-Day Holiday for Eid ul Adha from May 26 to 29
item.description: The Sindh government has officially declared a four-day holiday on the occasion of Eid ul Adha, which will be celebrated on May 27, 2026. According to the government notification, all provincial gover...
